additionalPlanContext:
Upgrade this Maven WAR web application to target JDK 21 and deploy/run on Apache Tomcat 10.1 (Jakarta).

Current state:
- Packaging: WAR
- Uses Apache Wicket (configured via web.xml using org.apache.wicket.protocol.http.WicketFilter)
- Uses Hibernate + MySQL
- Has src/main/webapp/WEB-INF/jboss-web.xml setting context-root to /addressbook

Target runtime and constraints:
- Runtime container: Apache Tomcat 10.1 (Jakarta Servlet).
- Output artifact must remain a WAR deployable to Tomcat 10.1.
- Do NOT introduce Spring Boot.
- Keep changes minimal but correct; prioritize compatibility and successful deployment.

Required Jakarta migration tasks:
1) Migrate namespaces from javax.* to jakarta.* across the codebase and configuration:
   - javax.servlet -> jakarta.servlet
   - javax.persistence -> jakarta.persistence
   - javax.validation -> jakarta.validation
   - javax.annotation -> jakarta.annotation (if used)
2) Upgrade Apache Wicket to a Jakarta-compatible version (Wicket 10.x or newer) so that the WicketFilter works on Tomcat 10.1.
3) Upgrade Hibernate ORM from 5.2.x to Hibernate 6.x (Jakarta Persistence) and update any configuration properties if required.
4) Upgrade dependencies:
   - MySQL driver to com.mysql:mysql-connector-j 8.x
   - Log4j to a modern 2.x version
   - Remove obsolete javax.xml.bind JAXB hacks unless strictly required; use modern/Jakarta equivalents if needed.
5) Update build tooling for Java 21:
   - maven-compiler-plugin with <release>21</release>
   - Ensure other Maven plugins are compatible with Java 21

CSP / Static resources (Wicket strict CSP):
- After upgrading Wicket, verify that the login page CSS and Google Fonts are not blocked by Content Security Policy (CSP).
- If the response header contains a strict CSP like:
  default-src 'none'; script-src 'strict-dynamic' 'nonce-...'; style-src 'nonce-...'; font-src 'self';
  then update Wicket CSP configuration in com.arun.WicketApplication#init().
- Keep strict CSP enabled but whitelist CSS and Google font sources using Wicket API:
  getCspSettings().blocking().add(CSPDirective.STYLE_SRC, "'self'");
  getCspSettings().blocking().add(CSPDirective.STYLE_SRC, "https://fonts.googleapis.com");
  getCspSettings().blocking().add(CSPDirective.FONT_SRC, "https://fonts.gstatic.com");
- Do NOT add 'unsafe-inline' unless unavoidable.
- Update HTML to use https for Google Fonts.
- Move inline <style> rules into CSS files to stay compatible with nonce CSP.

REQUIRED web.xml migration (Tomcat 10.1 / Jakarta):
- Update src/main/webapp/WEB-INF/web.xml from Java EE namespace/schema (java.sun.com, web-app_3_0.xsd, version=3.0) to Jakarta Servlet 5.0:
  xmlns="https://jakarta.ee/xml/ns/jakartaee"
  xsi:schemaLocation="https://jakarta.ee/xml/ns/jakartaee https://jakarta.ee/xml/ns/jakartaee/web-app_5_0.xsd"
  version="5.0"
- Update XML encoding to UTF-8.
- Keep existing WicketFilter mapping:
  filter-class org.apache.wicket.protocol.http.WicketFilter
  init-param applicationClassName=com.arun.WicketApplication
  url-pattern=/*

Context path requirements (Tomcat):
- Keep jboss-web.xml unchanged.
- Ensure Tomcat context path is /addressbook by setting Maven build finalName to 'addressbook' so artifact is target/addressbook.war.

Deliverables:
- Updated pom.xml (properties, dependencies, plugins) for Java 21 + Tomcat 10.1
- Updated web.xml as described
- Keep jboss-web.xml unchanged
- List code refactors performed (javax->jakarta) + any API fixes for Wicket/Hibernate
- Deployment steps:
  mvn clean package
  copy target/addressbook.war to $TOMCAT_HOME/webapps/
  start Tomcat and verify UI loads at /addressbook

Validation:
Run: mvn -U -DskipTests clean package
